name: auth-bypass
description: Flask app with multiple access control and authentication vulnerabilities: broken access control, IDOR, weak credentials, debug header bypass, and information disclosure.

expected_findings.description: Admin profile endpoint accessible without admin role check. Any authenticated user can view admin data containing a fake secret key.
Admin users list endpoint returns all user credentials (username+password) without any access control check.
Insecure Direct Object Reference: any authenticated user can access admin's data by ID=1. No ownership verification on /api/data/<id>.
Insecure Direct Object Reference: any authenticated user can access user1's data by ID=10. No ownership verification.
Weak/default passwords accepted: admin/password123, user1/welcome1, analyst/analyst123
Hidden admin endpoint accessible with custom X-Debug-Token header set to 'argus-bypass-2026'. No session or authentication required.

notes: BROKEN_ACCESS_CONTROL and IDOR findings are the most reliably detectable by DAST tools. WEAK_CREDENTIALS requires credential-stuffing testing. AUTH_BYPASS on /flag requires custom header injection testing. HARDCODED_SECRET (Flask secret key 'super-secret-key-12345') excluded from expected findings as it requires source-code scanning (gitleaks/semgrep), not web URL scanning.
